```{r, fig.height=2, fig.width=12}
tableList = lapply(speciesACList, function(object) {
  object$species_type = paste0(object$species, "_", gsub(" ", "", object$species_cluster))
  table(object$type, object$species_type)
})

overlap.stats = lapply(tableList, OverlapStatistics)
nngaba = as.character(CorrespondingCluster(overlap.stats, cluster = "16_NNgaba")$ident2)
nngly = as.character(CorrespondingCluster(overlap.stats, cluster = "2_NNgly")$ident2)

BIGSEURAT$species_type2 = paste0(BIGSEURAT$species, "_", gsub(" ", "", BIGSEURAT$species_cluster))
nn_matrix = JSMatrix(table(BIGSEURAT$type, BIGSEURAT$species_type2))[c('16_NNgaba', '2_NNgly'),c(nngaba, nngly)]
JSHeatmap(nn_matrix) + 
  xlab('Yan et al. 2020')+
  ylab('Orthotypes')
```

## Inferring displacement

```{r}
BIGSEURAT$yan_type = BIGSEURAT$mapped.back
BIGSEURAT$yan_no = str_split_fixed(BIGSEURAT$yan_type, '_', 2)[,1]
overstats = OverlapStatistics(table(BIGSEURAT$type, BIGSEURAT$yan_no))

displaced.types = c(7,2,54,36,39,32,17,21,63,37,44,46,48)

# Clusters corresponding to KT2, 16
overstats[overstats$ident2 %in% displaced.types & overstats$log.p > 20,]
BIGSEURAT$displaced = ifelse(BIGSEURAT$type %in% overstats[overstats$ident2 %in% displaced.types & overstats$log.p > 20,]$ident1, TRUE, FALSE)
Metadata(BIGSEURAT, 'type', 'displaced')

BIGSEURAT$type = paste0(BIGSEURAT$type, ifelse(BIGSEURAT$displaced, '*', ''))
BIGSEURAT$type = factor(BIGSEURAT$type, levels = Metadata(BIGSEURAT, 'dendro.order.type', 'type')$type)
BIGSEURAT$yan_type = ifelse(BIGSEURAT$yan_no %in% displaced.types, paste0(BIGSEURAT$yan_type, '*'), BIGSEURAT$yan_type)
```

All types

```{r, fig.height=7, fig.width=12}

ggsave('../../figures/my_figs/figS_Yan-comparison.pdf', height=7, width=12)
```

```{r, fig.height=2.5, fig.width=4.5}
new_tab$V1 = 1:42
new_tab$`Proportion displaced` = round(as.numeric(Metadata(ac.ortho, 'orthotype', 'prop.displaced')$prop.displaced), 2)
write.csv(new_tab, 'metadata/table1.csv')
```

Proportion displaced based on Choi et al. Fig 4D

```{r}
prop.displaced = fread('../../reference_files/Choi_fig4d_automeris.csv')[1:63,] %>% setNames(c('type', 'proportion'))
prop.displaced
prop.displaced$type = paste0('AC-', 1:63)
prop.displaced$proportion = round(prop.displaced$proportion / 1.55 * 0.8, 2) # 1.55 is calibration for 0.8

# Correct for defect with AC-63 (it's all displaced!)
prop.displaced$proportion[prop.displaced$type == 'AC-63'] = 1

prop.displaced
saveRDS(prop.displaced, '../../reference_files/Choi_fig4d_automeris.rds')
```

```{r, fig.height=5, fig.width=10}
BIGSEURAT = readRDS('../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateAC_BIGSEURAT.rds')
prop.displaced = readRDS('../../reference_files/Choi_fig4d_automeris.rds')

ot2ms = ConfusionMatrix(BIGSEURAT$type_no, BIGSEURAT$yan_type, plot = FALSE)
ot.prop.displaced = as.matrix(ot2ms) %*% as.numeric(as.matrix(prop.displaced)[,2])
barplot(t(ot.prop.displaced))
BIGSEURAT$prop.displaced = ot.prop.displaced[match(BIGSEURAT$type_no, rownames(ot.prop.displaced))]
ggbarplot(Metadata(BIGSEURAT, 'type', 'type_no', 'prop.displaced'), x = 'type', y = 'prop.displaced')+
  RotatedAxis()

# Add to object
unique(BIGSEURAT$type[BIGSEURAT$prop.displaced >= 0.20])
PrettyHistogram(Metadata(BIGSEURAT, 'type', 'prop.displaced')$prop.displaced, vline = 0.2)
BIGSEURAT$type = forcats::fct_recode(BIGSEURAT$type, "33_CA1*" = "33_CA1")
BIGSEURAT$type = forcats::fct_recode(BIGSEURAT$type, "40*" = "40")
BIGSEURAT$displaced[BIGSEURAT$prop.displaced > 0.2] = TRUE
unique(BIGSEURAT$type[BIGSEURAT$displaced])
saveRDS(BIGSEURAT, "../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateAC_BIGSEURAT.rds")
```

# Identification of orthotypes across species

## Jaccard similarity

```{r, fig.width=20, fig.height=35}
# heatmapList = lapply(speciesList, function(this.species) TitlePlot(ConfusionMatrix2(subset(BIGSEURAT, species == this.species)$type, subset(BIGSEURAT, species == this.species)$species_type), title = this.species))
# 
# # Plot
# ggarrange(plotlist = heatmapList, 
#           ncol = 3, 
#           nrow = 6, 
#           common.legend = TRUE, 
#           legend = "right")

SplitJaccardMatrix(BIGSEURAT, group.by = 'species', ident.1 = 'type', ident.2 = 'species_cluster', nrow=6, ncol=3)
```

## Determine conservation of each orthotype

```{r}
BIGSEURAT = ac.ortho
speciesACList = lapply(unique(BIGSEURAT$species), function(currentSpecies) subset(BIGSEURAT, species == currentSpecies))
names(speciesACList) = unique(BIGSEURAT$species)

# Generate jaccard matrices
jaccardList = lapply(seq_along(speciesACList), function(index) JSMatrix(t(table(speciesACList[[index]]$species_cluster, speciesACList[[index]]$type))))
names(jaccardList) = unique(BIGSEURAT$species)
saveRDS(jaccardList, '../../Ortho_Objects/jaccardList.rds')
```

```{r OT-conservation-boxplot, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=12}
jaccardList = lapply(jaccardList, as.data.frame.matrix)

# Get the best match in each species 
bestMatch = lapply(jaccardList, function(matrix) apply(matrix, 1, function(x) colnames(matrix)[which.max(x)]))
# reducedList = Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "row.names", all = T), jaccardList[1:3])
bestMatchDf = as.data.frame(t(do.call(rbind, bestMatch)))
colnames(bestMatchDf) = speciesList.liz
bestMatchDf$OrthoType = rownames(bestMatchDf)
saveRDS(bestMatchDf, '../../Ortho_Objects/best-match.rds')

bestJaccard = lapply(jaccardList, function(matrix) apply(matrix, 1, max))
# reducedList = Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "row.names", all = T), jaccardList[1:3])
bestJaccardDf = as.data.frame(t(do.call(rbind, bestJaccard)))
colnames(bestJaccardDf) = speciesList.liz
bestJaccardDf$OrthoType = rownames(bestJaccardDf)
saveRDS(bestJaccardDf, '../../Ortho_Objects/best-jaccard.rds')

PrettyHistogram(unlist(as.vector(select(bestMatchDf, -OrthoType))))

# Number of species with best jaccard over 0.2
n.present = apply(select(bestMatchDf, -OrthoType), 1, function(x) length(which(x > 0.2)))

# Melt and plot
melted = reshape2::melt(bestMatchDf)
plt = ggplot(subset(melted, variable != 'Ferret'), aes(x = reorder(OrthoType, value, FUN = median, decreasing = TRUE), y = value))+
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, linetype = "dashed")+
  stat_boxplot(aes(ymin = after_stat(lower), ymax = after_stat(upper)), outlier.shape = NA) +
  stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymin = ..ymax..), width = 0.5) +
  stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymax = ..ymin..), width = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = variable), color = 'black', shape = 21, position=position_jitter(0.2))+
  theme_cowplot() +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey", linewidth = 0.5))+
  xlab(NULL)+
  ylab("Presence score")+
  scale_color_discrete("Species")+
  RotatedAxis()
plt
```


```{r OT-conservation-boxplot, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=12}
pdf('../../figures/my_figs/ot-conservation-boxplot.pdf', height=4.5, width=12)
plt
dev.off()

# ggsave("../..OT-conservation-boxplot.pdf", device = "pdf", height=4.5, width=12, dpi = 300)
```

## Convergence analysis

```{r, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=12}
# Generate jaccard matrices
tableList = lapply(seq_along(speciesACList), function(index) table(speciesACList[[index]]$type, speciesACList[[index]]$species_cluster))
names(tableList) = speciesList.liz

# Checks
SimpsonIndex(tableList$Human['8_SAC*',], invert = TRUE)
SimpsonIndex(tableList$Rat['8_SAC*',], invert = TRUE)

# Compute simpson diversity index
simpson_list = lapply(tableList, function(x){
  apply(x, 1, function(x) {
    # check for at least 20 observations in the row
   if(sum(x) >= 20){
     SimpsonIndex(x, invert = TRUE)
   } else {
     NA
   }
  }) %>% data.frame %>% t
})

simpson_df = do.call(gtools::smartbind, simpson_list) %>% rownames_to_column('Species')
saveRDS(simpson_df, '../../Ortho_Objects/simpson-index.rds')

melted = reshape2::melt(simpson_df, id.vars = 'Species') %>% setNames(c('Species', 'Orthotype', 'Simpson index'))
# simpson_df$`Simpson index`[is.infinite(simpson_df$`Simpson index`)] = NA
plt = ggplot(na.omit(melted), aes(x = reorder(Orthotype, `Simpson index`, FUN = median, decreasing = FALSE), y = `Simpson index`))+
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, linetype = "dashed")+
  stat_boxplot(aes(ymin = after_stat(lower), ymax = after_stat(upper)), outlier.shape = NA) +
  stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymin = ..ymax..), width = 0.5) +
  stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymax = ..ymin..), width = 0.5) +
  # geom_violin(scale = 'width')+
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = Species), color = 'black', shape = 21, position=position_jitter(0.3))+
  theme_cowplot() +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey", size = 0.5))+
  # scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(add = 1))+
  xlab(NULL)+
  ylab("Simpson index")+
  scale_color_discrete("Species")+
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1,4))+
  # geom_hline(yintercept = c(1:5), linetype = 'dashed', linewidth = 0.5, color = 'grey', alpha = 0.8)+
  RotatedAxis()


plot_grid(plt + NoLegend(), 
          ggplot(melted, aes(y = `Simpson index`, after_stat(count)))+
            geom_density(fill = 'grey') + 
            coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1,4))+
            theme_cowplot()+
            RotatedAxis()+
            theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), 
                  axis.text.y = element_blank()), 
          rel_widths = c(9,1), 
          align = 'h', axis = 'bt'
)

ggsave("../../figures/my_figs/OT-simpson-boxplot.pdf", device = "pdf", height=4.5, width=12, dpi = 300)
```

## Confusion matrices by orthotype conservation

Josh's style (tight confusion matrices)

```{r orthotype-speciestype-tight, fig.height=3, fig.width=40}
OT_order = levels(reorder(melted$OrthoType, melted$value, FUN = median, decreasing = TRUE))
heatmapList2 = lapply(seq_along(speciesACList), function(index) {
  JSHeatmap(JSMatrix(t(table(speciesACList[[index]]$species_type, speciesACList[[index]]$type))), 
            heatmap = TRUE, 
            title = paste0(speciesList[[index]]), #title = paste0(unique(vertebrateAC$species)[[index]], '\n(ARI=', signif(adj.rand.index(speciesACList[[index]]$species_type, speciesACList[[index]]$type), 2), ')'), 
            row.order = OT_order, 
            stagger.threshold = 0.20) + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
                                              axis.title = element_blank(),
                                              axis.text.y = element_blank(), 
                                              plot.margin=grid::unit(c(0,0,0,0), "mm"), 
                                              axis.ticks = element_blank())
})

ggarrange(plotlist = heatmapList2, 
          ncol = (length(heatmapList2)), 
          nrow = 1, 
          common.legend = TRUE, 
          legend = "right",
          widths = sapply(speciesACList, function(object) length(unique(object$species_type))))
```

Find species types corresponding to orthotypes based on overlap statistics

```{r, eval = FALSE}
tableList = lapply(speciesACList, function(object) {
  object$species_type = paste0(object$species, "_", gsub(" ", "", object$species_type))
  table(object$type, object$species_type)
})

overlap.stats = lapply(tableList, OverlapStatistics)
# filtered.list = FilteredList(overlap.stats)

# Clusters corresponding to KT2, 16
CorrespondingCluster(overlap.stats, cluster = "16_KT2")
CorrespondingCluster(overlap.stats, cluster = "12")
CorrespondingCluster(overlap.stats, cluster = " 1")
```

## Pairwise comparisons

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=10}
# X = ConfusionMatrix(subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Human")$species_type, subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Human")$type)
# Y = ConfusionMatrix(subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Macaque")$type, subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Macaque")$species_type)

X = ConfusionMatrix(subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Mouse")$species_type, subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Mouse")$type)
Y = ConfusionMatrix(subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Rat")$type, subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Rat")$species_type)

Z = X %*% Y
JSHeatmap(JSMatrix(Z))

X = ConfusionMatrix(subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Mouse")$species_type, subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Mouse")$type)
Y = ConfusionMatrix(subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Rhabdomys")$type, subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Rhabdomys")$species_type)

Z = X %*% Y
JSHeatmap(JSMatrix(Z))

X = ConfusionMatrix(subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Mouse")$species_type, subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "Mouse")$type)
Y = ConfusionMatrix(subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "TreeShrew")$type, subset(BIGSEURAT, species == "TreeShrew")$species_type)

Z = X %*% Y
JSHeatmap(JSMatrix(Z))
```

# Extending to zebrafish and lamprey

```{r, fig.height=5, fig.width=15}
# For species used in SAMap subset, we will use the same cells
samap = fread('samap/pmzechliopsqhs_ac_seed_0_leiden_4.0.csv')[,-1] %>% setNames(c('UMAP_1', 'UMAP_2', 'annotated', 'species', 'cell', 'leiden_clusters'))
samap$cell = sub("-[^-]+$", "", samap$cell)
samap_subset = c('Human', 'Squirrel', "Opossum", 'Chicken', 'Lizard')
samap$leiden_clusters = factor(samap$leiden_clusters)
samap$species = convert_values(samap$species, data.frame(Original = c('hs', 'sq', "op", 'ch', 'li', 'pm', 'ze'), 
                                                         Converted = c('Human', 'Squirrel', "Opossum", 'Chicken', 'Lizard', 'Lamprey', 'Zebrafish')))
samap$oACs = BIGSEURAT$type[match(samap$cell, str_split_fixed(Cells(BIGSEURAT), '_', 2)[,2])]

plot_grid(PrettyUmap2(samap, group.by = 'leiden_clusters', min.segment.length = 0.05, pt.size = 0.01, cols = dtColors(length(unique(samap$leiden_clusters)))), 
          PrettyUmap2(samap, group.by = 'species', min.segment.length = 0.05, pt.size = 0.01, label = FALSE, show.legend = TRUE), 
          PrettyUmap2(samap[!is.na(samap$oACs)], group.by = 'oACs', min.segment.length = 0.05, pt.size = 0.01, cols = type_cols %>% setNames(levels(BIGSEURAT$type))), 
          nrow = 1)
```

```{r, fig.height=5, fig.width=7}
BIGSEURAT$samap_clusters = samap$leiden_clusters[match(str_split_fixed(Cells(BIGSEURAT), '_', 2)[,2], samap$cell)]

# ConfusionMatrix2(BIGSEURAT$type, BIGSEURAT$samap_clusters)

seurat2samap = JSMatrix(table(BIGSEURAT$type, BIGSEURAT$samap_clusters))
JSHeatmap2(BIGSEURAT$type, BIGSEURAT$samap_clusters, title = 'seurat2samap', stagger.threshold = 0.2)

samap2pm = JSMatrix(table(subset(samap, species == 'pm')$leiden_clusters, subset(samap, species == 'pm')$annotated))
JSHeatmap(samap2pm, title = 'samap2pm')
seurat2pm = seurat2samap %*% samap2pm
JSHeatmap(seurat2pm, title = 'seurat2pm')

samap2ze = JSMatrix(table(subset(samap, species == 'ze')$leiden_clusters, subset(samap, species == 'ze')$annotated))
JSHeatmap(samap2ze, title = 'samap2ze')
seurat2ze = seurat2samap %*% samap2ze
JSHeatmap(seurat2ze, title = 'seurat2ze')
```

# Leave one out cross validation of orthotypes

```{r}
vertebrateAC = readRDS('../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateAC_v2_100.rds')

dir.create('../../../storage/Ortho_Objects/cross_validation/')
lapply(c('Opossum', 'Chicken', 'Lizard'), function(this.species){
  object = subset(vertebrateAC, species == this.species, invert = TRUE)
  message('Using only these species: ', unique(object$species))
  object = ClusterSeurat(object, integrate.by = 'species', cluster_resolution = 1)
  saveRDS(object, paste0('../../../storage/Ortho_Objects/cross_validation/', this.species, '.rds'))
})
```

# Diversity analysis

## Measure 1: the inverse Simpson index

The Simpson index represents the probability of picking two items of the same group in a sample: $S = \sum p_i^2$ where $p_i$ is the proportion of group i. The inverse Simpson index is a commonly used measure of diversity, with the number representing the effective number of groups represented in your sample: $\frac{1}{S} = \frac{1}{\sum p_i^2}$. If they are all equally represented, then the inverse Simpson's index is equal to the number of groups: $\frac{1}{S} = \frac{1}{\sum p_i^2} = \frac{1}{i (\frac{1}{i})^2} = i$. 

```{r, eval = FALSE, fig.width=8, fig.height=4}
iSimpsonList = sapply(seq_along(speciesACList), function(index) {
  tableMatrix = table(speciesACList[[index]]$type)
  piList = tableMatrix/sum(tableMatrix)
  inverse.simpson = 1/sum(piList^2)
  return(inverse.simpson)
  }) 

iSimpsonDf = data.frame(iSimpson = iSimpsonList,
                        species = factor(unique(vertebrateAC$species), levels = unique(vertebrateAC$species)), 
                        nClusters = sapply(speciesACList, function(object) length(unique(object$species_type))))

# Plot
p1 = ggplot(iSimpsonDf, aes(x = species, y = iSimpson, label = species))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_text_repel()+ 
  xlab("Species")+
  ylab("OT inverse Simpson's index")+
  theme_cowplot()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank())

# Relationship to number of clusters
p2 = ScatterPlot(iSimpsonDf, 
                 x = "nClusters", 
                 y = "iSimpson", 
                 labels = "species", 
                 ylab = "inverse Simpson's index", 
                 xlab = "# of clusters found")+
  theme_cowplot()

ggarrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2, align = "h")
```

## Measure 2: number of clusters from unenriched samples, downsampled to an equal number of cells

```{r, eval = FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=5}
species_names = unique(vertebrateAC$species)

# Get largest unenriched batch
humanAC = readRDS("../../Species_Objects/HumanAC_v5.rds")
humanSubset = subset(humanAC, orig.file == "Hu220235OSmAll")

ratAC = readRDS("../../Species_Objects/RatAC_v5.rds")
# ratAC = subset(ratAC, donor == "Hu220235")
# ratAC = ClusterSeurat(ratAC, cluster_resolution = 1.5)

peromyscusAC = readRDS("../../Species_Objects/PeromyscusAC_v5.rds")
peromyscusSubset = subset(peromyscusAC, orig.file %in% c("Pero1CD73dpS1", "Pero1CD73dpS2", "Pero1CD73dpS3"))

# Downsample
downsampleTo = min(length(Cells(humanSubset)), length(Cells(peromyscusAC)))
peromyscusSubset = peromyscusSubset[, sample(colnames(peromyscusSubset), downsampleTo)]

# Cluster
humanSubset = ClusterSeurat(humanSubset, cluster_resolution = 1.5)
peromyscusSubset = ClusterSeurat(peromyscusSubset, cluster_resolution = 1.5)

p1 = plotClusterRes(humanSubset)
p2 = plotClusterRes(peromyscusSubset)

# res.df = cbind(resolution = p1$data$resolution, 
#                human = p1$data$nclusters, 
#                peromsycus = p2$data$nclusters)

res.df = rbind(p1$data, 
               p2$data)
res.df$species = rep(c("human", "peromyscus"), each = nrow(p1$data))

# res.df = reshape2::melt(res.df, id.vars = "resolution")
ggplot(res.df, aes(x = resolution, y = nclusters, color = species))+
    geom_line()+ 
    ylab("Number of clusters")+
    theme_bw()
```

# Expression correlation

```{r, eval = params$expr_corr}
vertebrateAC = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateAC_v3_100.rds")
```

## Correlation between types

```{r, eval = params$expr_corr, fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
# For consistency between RNA and integrated assay, we use same 2000 variable genes identified by integrated analysis
features.use = vertebrateAC@assays$integrated@var.features
```

```{r type-cor, eval = params$expr_corr, fig.height = 10, fig.width = 12}
avg_exp = AverageExpression(vertebrateAC, verbose = FALSE, group.by = "type", features = features.use)

type_cor = cor(as.data.frame(log1p(avg_exp$RNA)))
ha = RowAnnotation(subtype = Metadata(vertebrateAC, feature.1 = "seurat_clusters", feature.2 = "classification")$classification, annotation_name_side = "top", 
                       col = list(subtype = c("GABA" = "pink", "Gly" = "lightgreen")))
top_ha = HeatmapAnnotation(OT = colnames(type_cor), annotation_name_side = "left")

hm = Heatmap(type_cor, name = "cor", 
        # col = colorRamp2(c(0.5, 1), c("white", "red")), 
        cluster_rows = hclust(as.dist(1 - type_cor)), 
        cluster_columns = hclust(as.dist(1 - type_cor)), 
        show_column_names = TRUE, show_column_dend = FALSE, 
        column_title = "pairwise similarity between OTs (un-integrated)",
        show_row_names = TRUE, row_dend_side = "left", row_names_side = "left",
        heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Correlation"), 
        left_annotation = ha, top_annotation = top_ha)

ggarrange(grid.grabExpr(draw(hm)))

# ggsave("../../figures/my_figs/type-cor-1.png", height = 10, width = 12)

type_order = dendextend::order.hclust(hclust(as.dist(1 - type_cor)))

# Add dendrogram order to object
vertebrateAC$dendro.order = factor(vertebrateAC$seurat_clusters, levels = rev(type_order))
saveRDS(vertebrateAC, "../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateAC_v3_100.rds")
```


```{r type-cor-int, eval = params$expr_corr, fig.height = 10, fig.width = 12}
type_cor = cor(as.data.frame(log1p(avg_exp$integrated)))

hm = Heatmap(type_cor, name = "cor", 
        # col = colorRamp2(c(0.5, 1), c("white", "red")), 
        cluster_rows = hclust(as.dist(1 - type_cor)), 
        cluster_columns = hclust(as.dist(1 - type_cor)), 
        show_column_names = TRUE, show_column_dend = FALSE, 
        column_title = "pairwise similarity between OTs (integrated)",
        show_row_names = TRUE, row_dend_side = "left", row_names_side = "left",
        heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Correlation"), 
        top_annotation = ha)

ggarrange(grid.grabExpr(draw(hm)))

# ggsave("../../figures/my_figs/type-cor-int-1.png", height = 10, width = 12)
```

## Unintegrated

```{r type-cor-species-unint, eval = params$expr_corr,  fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
vertebrateAC$cluster_species = factor(paste0(vertebrateAC$seurat_clusters, "_", vertebrateAC$species), levels = sapply(type_order, function(x) paste0(x, "_", unique(vertebrateAC$species))))
avg_exp_species = AverageExpression(vertebrateAC, group.by = "cluster_species", features = features.use)
type_cor_species = cor(as.data.frame(log1p(avg_exp_species$RNA)))

ha = HeatmapAnnotation(species = str_split_fixed(colnames(type_cor_species), "_", 2)[,2], 
                       OT = str_split_fixed(colnames(type_cor_species), "_", 2)[,1],
                          annotation_name_side = "left")

hm = Heatmap(type_cor_species, name = "cor", 
        cluster_rows = FALSE, cluster_columns = FALSE,
        # col = colorRamp2(c(0.5, 1), c("white", "red")), 
        show_row_names = FALSE, show_column_names = FALSE, row_dend_side = "right", 
        show_column_dend = TRUE, column_title = "unintegrated data correlations",
        heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Correlation"), 
        top_annotation = ha)
ggarrange(grid.grabExpr(draw(hm)))

# ggsave("../../figures/my_figs/type-cor-species-unint-1.png", height = 10, width = 12)
```

## Gene expression trees

Try genes expressed in every species

```{r, eval = params$expr_corr}
vertebrateAC.list = SplitObject(vertebrateAC, split.by = "species")
expressedGenes.list = lapply(vertebrateAC.list, function(object){
  return(rownames(object@assays$RNA@counts)[rowSums(object@assays$RNA@counts)>0])
})

common_genes = Reduce(intersect, expressedGenes.list)
```

```{r, fig.width=6, eval = params$expr_corr, fig.height=5}
                   group.by = "species",
```


## Split by phylogenetic group

```{r, fig.height=6, fig.width=6}
# vertebrateAC = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateAC_v2_100.rds")
# vertebrateAC$clusters = factor(as.numeric(vertebrateAC$seurat_clusters), levels = 1:nClusters(vertebrateAC))
ac.ortho$orthotype.no = factor(ac.ortho$orthotype.no, levels = 1:42)
vertebrateAC = ac.ortho #readRDS('../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateAC_BIGSEURAT.rds')
# vertebrateAC$dendro.order = vertebrateAC$orthotype.no

# Scale data for heatmap
PrimateAC = DownsampleSeurat(subset(vertebrateAC, species %in% c("Human", "Macaque", "Marmoset", "MouseLemur")), group.by = 'seurat_clusters', size = 50)
PrimateAC = ScaleData(PrimateAC, features = rownames(vertebrateAC))
RodentAC = DownsampleSeurat(subset(vertebrateAC, species %in% c("Mouse", "Rhabdomys", "Rat", "Peromyscus", "Squirrel")), group.by = 'seurat_clusters', size = 50)
RodentAC = ScaleData(RodentAC, features = rownames(vertebrateAC))
LaurasiaAC = DownsampleSeurat(subset(vertebrateAC, species %in% c("Ferret", "Pig", "Sheep", "Cow")), group.by = 'seurat_clusters', size = 50)
LaurasiaAC = ScaleData(LaurasiaAC, features = rownames(vertebrateAC))
```

```{r}
logFC.threshold = 0.6
padj.threshold = 0.05

# Primate DEGs
PrimateACMarkers = wilcoxauc(PrimateAC, 'seurat_clusters')

# Rodent DEGs
RodentACMarkers = wilcoxauc(RodentAC, 'seurat_clusters')

# Laurasiatherian DEGs
LaurasiaACMarkers = wilcoxauc(LaurasiaAC, 'seurat_clusters')

# Extract top genes
PrimateDEGs = lapply((levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order)), function(cluster){
  subset = subset(PrimateACMarkers, group == as.numeric(cluster) & logFC > logFC.threshold & padj < padj.threshold) %>% arrange(padj) 
  return(subset)
})

RodentDEGs = lapply((levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order)), function(cluster){
  subset = subset(RodentACMarkers, group == as.numeric(cluster) & logFC > logFC.threshold & padj < padj.threshold) %>% arrange(padj) 
  return(subset)
})

LaurasiaDEGs = lapply((levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order)), function(cluster){
  subset = subset(LaurasiaACMarkers, group == as.numeric(cluster) & logFC > logFC.threshold & padj < padj.threshold) %>% arrange(padj) 
  return(subset)
})

# Conserved and species-enriched genes
sharedDEGs = lapply(seq_along((levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))), function(element){
  intersection = Reduce(intersect, list(PrimateDEGs[[element]]$feature, RodentDEGs[[element]]$feature, LaurasiaDEGs[[element]]$feature))
  }) %>% setNames(levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))

primateSpecificDEGs = lapply(seq_along((levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))), function(element){
  speciesDEGs = PrimateDEGs[[element]]$feature
  return(speciesDEGs[!speciesDEGs %in% c(RodentDEGs[[element]]$feature, LaurasiaDEGs[[element]]$feature)])
  }) %>% setNames(levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))

rodentSpecificDEGs = lapply(seq_along((levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))), function(element){
  speciesDEGs = RodentDEGs[[element]]$feature
  return(speciesDEGs[!speciesDEGs %in% c(PrimateDEGs[[element]]$feature, LaurasiaDEGs[[element]]$feature)])
  }) %>% setNames(levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))

laurasiaSpecificDEGs = lapply(seq_along((levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))), function(element){
  speciesDEGs = LaurasiaDEGs[[element]]$feature
  return(speciesDEGs[!speciesDEGs %in% c(RodentDEGs[[element]]$feature, PrimateDEGs[[element]]$feature)])
  }) %>% setNames(levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))

# Trim some laura genes, theres too many for some clusters
# laurasiaSpecificDEGs = lapply(laurasiaSpecificDEGs, function(x) head(x, 150))
  
lens = list(length(unique(sharedDEGs %>% unlist)), 
            length(unique(primateSpecificDEGs %>% unlist)),
            length(unique(rodentSpecificDEGs %>% unlist)), 
            length(unique(laurasiaSpecificDEGs %>% unlist)))
cumsum(lens)

# message("# of shared genes found: ", )
# message("# of primate genes found: ", length(unique(primateSpecificDEGs %>% unlist)))
# message("# of rodent genes found: ", length(unique(rodentSpecificDEGs %>% unlist)))
# message("# of laura genes found: ", length(unique(laurasiaSpecificDEGs %>% unlist)))

# Check for overlap
primate_de = lapply(PrimateDEGs, function(x) x$feature)
rodent_de = lapply(RodentDEGs, function(x) x$feature)
laurasia_de = lapply(LaurasiaDEGs, function(x) x$feature)

# median(sapply(1:42, function(i) VennDiagram(primate_de[[i]], rodent_de[[i]])$pval))
# median(sapply(1:42, function(i) VennDiagram(primate_de[[i]], laurasia_de[[i]])$pval))
# median(sapply(1:42, function(i) VennDiagram(rodent_de[[i]], laurasia_de[[i]])$pval))

length(which(sapply(1:42, function(i) VennDiagram(primate_de[[i]], rodent_de[[i]])$pval) < 1e-3))
length(which(sapply(1:42, function(i) VennDiagram(primate_de[[i]], laurasia_de[[i]])$pval) < 1e-3))
length(which(sapply(1:42, function(i) VennDiagram(rodent_de[[i]], laurasia_de[[i]])$pval) < 1e-3))

Heatmap2(outer(primate_de, rodent_de, FUN = Vectorize(jaccard)))
Heatmap2(outer(primate_de, laurasia_de, FUN = Vectorize(jaccard)))
Heatmap2(outer(rodent_de, laurasia_de, FUN = Vectorize(jaccard)))
```

```{r, fig.height=9, fig.width=27}
ht_wrapper = function(object, color, list, add.rectangles = FALSE){
  grid.grabExpr(
    # draw(
      SeuratHeatmap(object, #DownsampleSeurat(object, group.by = 'dendro.order', size = 50), 
                    group.by = 'dendro.order', 
                    features = unlist(list), 
                    color =c('white', 'white', color),
                    show_column_names = FALSE, 
                    show_row_names = FALSE, 
                    show_heatmap_legend = FALSE, 
                    border = FALSE, 
                    rotate = TRUE,
                    shuffle = TRUE, 
                    add.rectangles = add.rectangles, 
                    row.breaks = as.character(object$dendro.order[order(object$dendro.order)]), #object$dendro.order,
                    col.breaks = rep(names(list), lengths(list)), 
                    lty = 2)#, 
      # padding = unit(c(0,0,0,0), "mm")
      # )
  )
}

# Shared
h1 = ht_wrapper(PrimateAC, 'deeppink', sharedDEGs, add.rectangles = F)
h2 = ht_wrapper(RodentAC, 'orange2', sharedDEGs, add.rectangles = F)
h3 = ht_wrapper(LaurasiaAC, 'chartreuse3', sharedDEGs, add.rectangles = F)

# Primate
l1 = ht_wrapper(PrimateAC, 'deeppink', primateSpecificDEGs, add.rectangles = F)
l2 = ht_wrapper(RodentAC, 'deeppink', primateSpecificDEGs)
l3 = ht_wrapper(LaurasiaAC, 'deeppink', primateSpecificDEGs)

# Rodent
m1 = ht_wrapper(PrimateAC, 'orange2', rodentSpecificDEGs)
m2 = ht_wrapper(RodentAC, 'orange2', rodentSpecificDEGs, add.rectangles = F)
m3 = ht_wrapper(LaurasiaAC, 'orange2', rodentSpecificDEGs)

# Laurasia
r1 = ht_wrapper(PrimateAC, 'chartreuse3', laurasiaSpecificDEGs)
r2 = ht_wrapper(RodentAC, 'chartreuse3', laurasiaSpecificDEGs)
r3 = ht_wrapper(LaurasiaAC, 'chartreuse3', laurasiaSpecificDEGs, add.rectangles = F)
```


```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=27}
# Plot
buff.x = 5
buff.y = 0.02
bias = 1000
plot_grid(plotlist = list(h1, NULL, l1, NULL, m1, NULL, r1, 
                          NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL,
                           h2, NULL, l2, NULL, m2, NULL, r2, 
                          NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL,
                           h3, NULL, l3, NULL, m3, NULL, r3), #function(x) grid.grabExpr(draw(x))), 
          ncol = 7, nrow = 5, 
          rel_widths = c(length(unique(sharedDEGs %>% unlist)) + bias, buff.x,
                         length(unique(primateSpecificDEGs %>% unlist)) + bias, buff.x,
                         length(unique(rodentSpecificDEGs %>% unlist)) + bias, buff.x,
                         length(unique(laurasiaSpecificDEGs %>% unlist)) + bias),
          # rel_widths = c(1, buff.x, 
          #                1, buff.x, 
          #                1, buff.x, 
          #                1), 
          rel_heights = c(1, buff.y, 1, buff.y, 1))

ggsave('../../figures/my_figs/fig-cons-heatmap-3.png', height=8, width=26, dpi = 100)
```

## Only for known types

```{r}
types.use = rev(c("SAC", "A17", "CA1", "A2", "A8", "SEG", "VG3"))

# Subset to known types
metadata = Metadata(vertebrateAC, feature.1 = "seurat_clusters", feature.2 = "lit_type")
metadata.ordered = metadata[metadata$lit_type %in% types.use,] %>% arrange(factor(lit_type, levels = types.use))
types.clusters = metadata.ordered$seurat_clusters
names(types.clusters) = paste0(metadata.ordered$seurat_clusters, "_", metadata.ordered$lit_type)
```

## Venn diagrams

```{r, eval = FALSE, fig.height=3, fig.width=3}
vd_list = lapply(seq_along(types.clusters), function(index){
  cluster = types.clusters[[index]]
  VennDiagram(PrimateDEGs[[cluster]]$feature, RodentDEGs[[cluster]]$feature, LaurasiaDEGs[[cluster]]$feature, 
              filename = paste0(names(types.clusters)[[index]], "_vd.png"))
})

vd_list

par(mfrow = c(1,2))
plot(c(1,2,3), c(1,2,4))
plot(c(1,2,3), c(1,2,4))
```

## Gene ontology 

```{r, eval = FALSE, fig.height= 9, fig.width=12}
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

geneLists = lapply(list(sharedDEGs, primateSpecificDEGs, rodentSpecificDEGs, laurasiaSpecificDEGs), function(x) sort(unique(unlist(x))))
names(geneLists) = c("shared", "primates", "rodents", "laurasiatherians")

GO.results = lapply(geneLists, function(geneList){
  enrichGO(gene = geneList,
                # universe      = names(geneList),
                OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
                keyType       = 'SYMBOL',
                ont           = "BP",
                pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                pvalueCutoff  = 0.01,
                qvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
                readable      = TRUE)
})

GO.plots = lapply(GO.results, function(ego) dotplot(ego, showCategory=10) + scale_fill_gradient(low = "magenta", high = "turquoise"))

ggarrange(plotlist = GO.plots, nrow = 2, ncol =2, labels = names(geneLists), hjust = 0, align = "v")
```

```{r, eval = FALSE}
png("venn_diagrams.png", width = 14, height = 2)
par(mfrow = c(1,7))
plot(vd_list[[1]], labels=F, quantities=list(cex=1.8), fills=c("blue","turquoise","orange"))
plot(vd_list[[2]], labels=F, quantities=list(cex=1.8), fills=c("blue","turquoise","orange"))
dev.off()
```

```{r}
knownTypesAC = subset(vertebrateAC, lit_type %in% types.use)
knownTypesAC$lit_type = factor(knownTypesAC$lit_type, levels = types.use)

# Primate DEGs
PrimateKnown = subset(knownTypesAC, species %in% c("Human", "Macaque", "Marmoset", "MouseLemur"))

# Rodent DEGs
RodentKnown = subset(knownTypesAC, species %in% c("Mouse", "Rat", "Rhabdomys", "Peromyscus", "Squirrel"))

# Laurasiatherian DEGs
LaurasiaKnown = subset(knownTypesAC, species %in% c("Ferret", "Pig", "Sheep", "Cow"))

sharedKnown = sharedDEGs[match(types.clusters, levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))]
primateSpecificKnown = primateSpecificDEGs[match(types.clusters, levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))]
rodentSpecificKnown = rodentSpecificDEGs[match(types.clusters, levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))]
laurasiaSpecificKnown = laurasiaSpecificDEGs[match(types.clusters, levels(vertebrateAC$dendro.order))]

PrimateSubsetKnown = subset(PrimateKnown, cells = WhichCells(PrimateAC, downsample = 50, seed = 12345))
RodentSubsetKnown = subset(RodentKnown, cells = WhichCells(RodentAC, downsample = 50, seed = 12345))
LaurasiaSubsetKnown = subset(LaurasiaKnown, cells = WhichCells(LaurasiaAC, downsample = 50, seed = 12345))
```

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=15, eval = FALSE}
LaurasiaSubset = subset(LaurasiaKnown, cells = WhichCells(LaurasiaAC, downsample = 50, seed = 12345))
```

# Maximum parsimony tree*


Observations:

* Glycinergic
* Family 3 e.g. Bnc2
* GABA family 1 e.g. TSHZ3
* Family 2 e.g. FOXP1/EOMES
* SAC-specific 

```{r}
pdf('tf_analysis/ac-tf-dotplots-ordered.pdf', height = 5, width = 9)
lapply(tfs_ordered, function(gene) geneDotPlotFast(ac.ortho, gene = gene, binarization = k.masks[[gene]]))
dev.off()
```

Clean it up more:

* Rotate 18 and 41 done
* Rotate 24 and 26 done

```{r}
# vertebrateAC = readRDS('../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateAC_v2_100.rds')

saveRDS(row_dend(hm), 'tf_analysis/tf_dendro.rds')
tf.dendrogram = readRDS('tf_analysis/tf_dendro.rds')
ac.dendrogram = readRDS('../../Ortho_Objects/ac.dendrogram.rds')
labels(ac.dendrogram) = levels(BIGSEURAT$type)
plot(ac.dendrogram)

library(dendextend)
ac.dendrogram2 = rotate(ac.dendrogram, order = c(labels(ac.dendrogram)[c(1,2,4,3)],labels(ac.dendrogram)[c(9,10,8,7,6,5)],labels(ac.dendrogram)[11:42]))
plot(ac.dendrogram2)
```

### Hyperparameter sweeps

Metrics: 
* Robustness score (bootstrap with same method/same parameters)
* Sensitivity to loss of any one TF (loo trees)
* Cross-consistency (other methods, other parameters)

```{r, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=20}
library(phangorn)
tf.expr.mask = readRDS("tf_analysis/tf.k.masks.rds")
colnames(tf.expr.mask) = gsub('\\*', '', colnames(tf.expr.mask))

sweep = seq(30, nrow(tf.expr.mask), by = 2)
nTF.trees = lapply(sweep, function(nTF) 
  ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask)[,1:nTF], nIter = 100, mc.cores = 1, bootstrap = FALSE)) 
names(nTF.trees) = sweep

# Robinson fold distances
plot_grid(
  ggHeatmap(Iterate(lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$UPGMA), RF.dist), title = 'UPGMA'), 
  ggHeatmap(Iterate(lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$NJ), RF.dist), title = 'NJ'), 
  ggHeatmap(Iterate(lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$MP), RF.dist), title = 'MP'), 
  ggHeatmap(Iterate(lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$ML), RF.dist), title = 'ML'), 
  nrow = 1
)
```

Consistency across methods

```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
# ggHeatmap(Iterate(unlist(lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x[c('UPGMA', 'NJ', 'MP', 'ML')]), recursive = FALSE), RF.dist))

ggHeatmap(Iterate(c(
  upgma = lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$UPGMA), 
  nj = lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$NJ), 
  mp = lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$MP), 
  ml = lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$ML)
  ), RF.dist))
```
```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
# ggHeatmap(Iterate(unlist(lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x[c('UPGMA', 'NJ', 'MP', 'ML')]), recursive = FALSE), RF.dist))

ggHeatmap(Iterate(c(
  upgma = lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$UPGMA), 
  nj = lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$NJ), 
  mp = lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$MP), 
  ml = lapply(nTF.trees, function(x) x$ML)
  ), wRF.dist))
```

```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
ideal.nTF = '68'
plot(nTF.trees[[ideal.nTF]]$UPGMA, type = 'unrooted')
plot(nTF.trees[[ideal.nTF]]$NJ, type = 'unrooted')
plot(nTF.trees[[ideal.nTF]]$MP, type = 'unrooted')
plot(nTF.trees[[ideal.nTF]]$ML, type = 'unrooted')
```

## Maximum parsimony tree construction 

Branch length assignment using ACCTRAN

```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
tf.expr.mask = readRDS("tf_analysis/tf.k.masks.rds")
final.trees = ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask), root = 47, mc.cores = 10, nIter = 100)
plot(final.trees$MP, type="phylogram", cex = 1, digits = 2)
nodelabels(final.trees$MP$node.label)

# Can't trust phangorn for bootstrap support values; not agnostic to root!

# Save reference tree
saveRDS(final.trees$MP, 'tf_analysis/mp-tree-unrooted.rds')
final.trees$MP$node.label = NULL
write.tree(unroot(final.trees$MP), file = "tf_analysis/mp-tree-unrooted.nwk")

# Save bootstrap trees
bs_trees = BootstrapParsimonyTrees(t(tf.expr.mask), mc.cores = 10)
write.tree(bs_trees, file = "tf_analysis/mp-bootstrap.tre")

system('iqtree2 -t tf_analysis/mp-bootstrap.tre --support tf_analysis/mp-tree-unrooted.nwk')
```


```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
# bs.tree = ape::read.tree('tf_analysis/mp-bootstrap.tre.suptree')
# bs.tree$bs.support = bs.tree$node.label
# bs.tree$node.label = (length(bs.tree$tip.label)+1):((length(bs.tree$tip.label)+bs.tree$Nnode))
# # bs.tree = root(bs.tree, node = 73)
# plot(bs.tree, type="phylogram", cex = 1, digits = 2)
# nodelabels(bs.tree$bs.support)

bs.tree = ape::read.tree('tf_analysis/mp-bootstrap.tre.suptree')
bs.tree = root(bs.tree, node = 73)
bs.tree$bs.support = map_support_to_edges(bs.tree)
plot(bs.tree, type="phylogram", cex = 1, digits = 2)
edgelabels(bs.tree$bs.support)
```

### Leave one out support

```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
bs_trees = LOOParsimonyTrees(t(tf.expr.mask), mc.cores = 10)
write.tree(bs_trees, file = "tf_analysis/mp-loo.tre")

system('iqtree2 -t tf_analysis/mp-loo.tre --support tf_analysis/mp-tree-unrooted.nwk --prefix tf_analysis/mp-tree-loo')
```

```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
# bs.tree$bs.support = bs.tree$node.label
# bs.tree$node.label = (length(bs.tree$tip.label)+1):((length(bs.tree$tip.label)+bs.tree$Nnode))
# bs.tree = root(bs.tree, node = 73)
# plot(bs.tree, type="phylogram", cex = 1, digits = 2)
# nodelabels(bs.tree$bs.support)

bs.tree = ape::read.tree('tf_analysis/mp-tree-loo.suptree')
plotBS(bs.tree, type = "phylogram")
bs.tree = root(bs.tree, node = 73, edgelabel = TRUE)
plotBS(bs.tree, type = "phylogram")
bs.tree$bs.support = map_support_to_edges(bs.tree)
plot(bs.tree, type="phylogram", cex = 1, digits = 2)
edgelabels(bs.tree$bs.support)

# Compare to phangorn
bs_trees = read.tree(file = "tf_analysis/mp-loo.tre")
plotBS(bs.tree, bs_trees, type = "phylogram")
```

```{r, fig.height=12, fig.width=8}
  NFI_family = rev(c("NR2F2","NFIX", "NFIB", "NEUROD4", "SATB2", "EBF2",
                 "EBF3", "ST18", "PROX1", "EBF1", "PRDM13")),
  Neurod2_family = c("NEUROD2", "TFAP2B", 'TFAP2A'),
  A2 = c("ONECUT1", "NFIA"),
  VG3_family = c( "FOXP2", "BNC2", "TFAP2E", "NR1H4", "LHX2", "BNC1")
)

Heatmap(t(tf.expr.mask)[,unlist(glycinergic_families)], #[levels(BIGSEURAT$type),], 
        row_order = gsub('\\*', '', levels(ac.ortho$type)),
        rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 1),
        border_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 1),
        col = c("lightgrey", "#584B9FFF"), 
        # show_row_dend = TRUE,
        # show_column_dend = TRUE, 
        cluster_rows = mp.tree, #phytools::force.ultrametric(mp.tree, method = "extend"),
        cluster_columns = FALSE,
        # clustering_distance_rows = "manhattan", 
        # clustering_method_rows = "average", 
        # clustering_distance_columns = "manhattan",
        # clustering_method_columns = "average",
        show_heatmap_legend = FALSE)

# Dotplot
ac.ortho$type_clean = factor(gsub('\\*', '', ac.ortho$type_unordered), 
                             levels = gsub('\\*', '', levels(ac.ortho$type_unordered)))
ClusteredDotplot2(ac.ortho, 
                  features = unlist(glycinergic_families), 
                  group.by = 'type_clean', 
                  cluster_columns = mp.tree, 
                  max.pct = 50)

genes.omit = c('HLF', 'RORB', 'ZFPM2', 'ZNF503', 'FOXP1', 'DACH1', 'CUX2', 'KLF7', 'ZNF488', 'NR2F2', 'NR2F1', 'PBX1', 'ZNF521', 'GBX2', 'POU3F3', 'ZBTB18', 'FEZF1', 'FOXN2')
features.use = setdiff(rownames(tf.expr.mask), genes.omit)
ClusteredDotplot2(ac.ortho, 
                  features = features.use, 
                  group.by = 'type_clean', 
                  cluster_rows = hclust(dist(tf.expr.mask[features.use,], method = 'manhattan')),
                  cluster_columns = mp.tree,
                  max.pct = 50, 
                  dot.scale.factor = 0.3, 
                  pseudocount = 5, 
                  rotate = FALSE, 
                  show_row_dend = FALSE)
```

### Reconstruction of developmental sequence

```{r, fig.height=6.5, fig.width=6.5}
anc.acctran <- ancestral.pars(treeRooted, phydat, "ACCTRAN")
# anc.mpr <- ancestral.pars(treeRooted, phydat, "MPR")

pdf("tf_analysis/reconstruction_v3_acctran.pdf", height = 6.5, width = 6.5)
for(gene in tf.order){
  # plotAnc(treeLabeled, anc.mpr, which(tf.order == gene), type = "phylogram", cex.pie = 0.4, col = c("white", "black"))
  plotAnc(treeRooted, anc.acctran, which(tf.order == gene), type = "phylogram", cex.pie = 0.3, col = c("white", "black"), cex = 0.6, show.node.label = FALSE)
  title(gene)
}
dev.off()
```

Find TF switches

```{r, fig.height=30, fig.width=20}
gained = apply(treeRooted$edge, 1, function(row){
  gained = tf.order[which(ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[2]]][,2]) - ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2]) > 0)] #& anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2] == 0)] # an increase from 0 (not expressed)
  if(length(gained) > 0){
    gained = paste0(gained, "+")
    paste0(gained, collapse = "\n")
  } else {
    NA
  }
})

lost = apply(treeRooted$edge, 1, function(row){
  lost = tf.order[which(ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[2]]][,2]) - ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2]) < 0)] #& anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2] == 1)] # a decrease from 1 (expressed)
  if(length(lost) > 0){
    lost = paste0(lost, "-")
    paste0(lost, collapse = "\n")
  } else {
    NA
  }
})

# Plot gained TFs
pdf("tf_analysis/labeled_tree_gained_lost.pdf", height=30, width=20)
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = TRUE)
edgelabels(gained, srt = 0, bg = "green")

# Plot lost TFs
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = TRUE)
edgelabels(lost, srt = 0, bg = "red")
dev.off()

# Plot all TF switches
switches = gsub('^\n|\n$', '', gsub("NA", "", paste0(gained, "\n", lost)))
switches[switches == "\n"] = NA

pdf("tf_analysis/labeled_tree_v3.pdf", height = 30, width = 20)
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = FALSE)
edgelabels(switches, srt = 0, bg = "white")
add.scale.bar()
dev.off()
```

Advanced visualization with metadata: 
* Boostrap support values
* Expression of each TF (branch color or point or node annotation)
* GABA/gly (annotation)

```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
library(ggtree)
library(treeio)

# Seed for tree layout
set.seed(5)
tree = as.treedata(treeRooted)

# Add metadata
metadata = as.tibble(Metadata(vertebrateAC, feature.1 = "type", feature.2 = "classification") %>% setNames(c("label", "classification")))
expression = as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, lapply(anc.acctran, function(x) as.character(x[,2]))) %>% magrittr::set_colnames(tf.order))
expression$label = rownames(expression)
bootstrap = data.frame(label = as.character(treeRooted$node.label), 
                      bootstrap = treeBS$node.label)
anno_tree = list(as.tibble(tree), as.tibble(expression), metadata, bootstrap) %>% purrr::reduce(full_join, by = "label") 

# Names
anno_tree$name = anno_tree$label
anno_tree$name[ncol(tf.expr.mask):length(anno_tree$name)] = ""

saveRDS(anno_tree, "tf_analysis/ac_anno_tree.rds")

# GABA vs gly
ggtree(as.treedata(anno_tree), layout = "equal_angle") + 
  geom_tippoint(aes(color=classification))+
  # geom_nodepoint(aes(color=TCF4))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("black", "red"))+
  # geom_tiplab(geom = "text")+
  geom_text_repel(aes(label=name), max.overlaps = Inf, show.legend = FALSE, seed = 42)+
  geom_treescale()

# Bootstrap support values
ggtree(as.treedata(anno_tree), layout = "equal_angle") + 
  geom_nodepoint(aes(color=bootstrap), size = 4)+
  # geom_label(aes(label = bootstrap))+
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "black", high ="red", mid = "yellow")+
  # geom_tiplab(geom = "text")+
  geom_text_repel(aes(label=name), max.overlaps = Inf, show.legend = FALSE, seed = 42)+
  geom_treescale()
```

```{r}
pdf("tf_analysis/prettyReconstruction.pdf", height = 8, width = 8)
for(gene in tf.order){
  print(TitlePlot(PrettyTree(anno_tree, gene), gene))
}
dev.off()
```

## Show that tree topology is biologically relevant

Summarize key switch events with metrics:

* Gini impurity (likelihood of being misclassified if it were assigned that class): $1 - \sum p_i^2$
* Monophyletic TFs
* Parsimony scores of trees
* Mean bootstrap support

```{r}

# Easy example: TCF4
# descendants = treeRooted$tip.label[Descendants(treeRooted, 48)[[1]]]
# others = treeRooted$tip.label[-Descendants(treeRooted, 48)[[1]]]
# descendants_expr = anno_tree$TCF4[anno_tree$label %in% descendants]
# others_expr = anno_tree$TCF4[anno_tree$label %in% others]
# Gini impurity calculation
# gini = gini_impurities(data.table(label = c(rep("yes", length(descendants)), rep("no", length(others))), 
#                                   expr = c(descendants_expr, others_expr)))[2,3]
# gini = (gini_impurity(descendant_expr) * length(descendants) + gini_impurity(others_expr) * length(others))/(length(others) + length(descendants))

library(mltools)

# Find all TF switches
tf.switch.summary = lapply(seq_len(nrow(treeRooted$edge)), function(index){
  row = treeRooted$edge[index,]
  gained = tf.order[which(anc.acctran[[row[2]]][,2] - anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2] > 0)]
  lost = tf.order[which(anc.acctran[[row[2]]][,2] - anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2] < 0)]
  return(gained) #c(paste0(gained, "+"), paste0(lost, "-")))
})
names(tf.switch.summary) = treeRooted$edge[,2]

# Gini impurity of TFs
tf.gini.summary = do.call(rbind, lapply(names(tf.switch.summary), function(node){
  
  # TF switches at node
  tfs = tf.switch.summary[[node]]
  
  # Descendants
  descendants = treeRooted$tip.label[Descendants(treeRooted, as.numeric(node))[[1]] ]
  
  lapply(tfs, function(tf){
    
    data = data.table(label = orthotypes %in% descendants, expr = as.character(tf.expr.mask[tf,]))
    data.frame(tf = tf, 
               node = node, 
               gini = as.numeric(gini_impurities(data)[2,3]))
  })
  
}) %>% unlist(recursive = FALSE))

PrettyHistogram(tf.gini.summary$gini)
tf.gini.summary %>% arrange(gini)
```

Plot just the most informative switches

```{r, fig.height=20, fig.width=20}
# tf.switch.summary.filt = lapply(seq_along(tf.switch.summary), function(index){
#   tf.switch.summary[[index]][names(tf.switch.summary)[[index]] ]
# })
tf.switch.summary.filt = lapply(names(tf.switch.summary), function(n) subset(tf.gini.summary, node == n & gini < 0.2)$tf)
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = TRUE)
edgelabels(lapply(tf.switch.summary.filt, paste0, collapse = "\n"), srt = 0, bg = "green")
```

Monophyletic switches

```{r}
FindMonophyletic(tf.expr.mask, treeRooted, exclude.single = TRUE) %>% which %>% length
FindMonophyletic(tf.expr.mask, treeRooted, exclude.single = FALSE) %>% which
```
Trees from shuffled matrix

```{r, fig.height=7, fig.width=12}
nIter = 30

results.row = do.call(rbind, mclapply(seq_len(nIter), function(iter){
  
  set.seed(iter)
  
  # Shuffle rows (genes): preserves number of OTs in which each TF is expressed in
  rand.expr.mask = t(apply(tf.expr.mask, 1, function(row) sample(row)))
  colnames(rand.expr.mask) = colnames(tf.expr.mask)
  
  # Plot
  if(iter == 1) print(Heatmap(t(rand.expr.mask), 
        rect_gp = gpar(col = "grey", lwd = 1), col = c("white", "black"), 
        show_row_dend = FALSE, show_column_dend = FALSE))
  
  # Construct random tree
  rand.tree = MaxParsimonyTree(rand.expr.mask, minit = 1000)
  
  return(data.frame(iter = iter, 
                    parsimony = rand.tree$parsimony, 
                    nMonophyletic = length(which(rand.tree$monophyletic)), 
                    meanBS = mean(rand.tree$tree$node.label %>% replace_na(0)), 
                    nNas = length(which(is.na(rand.tree$tree$node.label)))
                    )
         )
}, mc.cores = min(c(nIter, 16))))
```

```{r}
results.col = do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nIter), function(iter){
  
  set.seed(iter)
  
  # Shuffle columns (OTs): preserves number of TFs expressed by each OT
  rand.expr.mask = apply(tf.expr.mask, 2, function(col) sample(col))
  rownames(rand.expr.mask) = rownames(tf.expr.mask)
  
  # Plot
  # Heatmap(rand.expr.mask, rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 1))
  
  # Construct random tree
  rand.tree = MaxParsimonyTree(rand.expr.mask)
  
  return(data.frame(iter = iter, 
                    parsimony = rand.tree$parsimony, 
                    nMonophyletic = length(which(rand.tree$monophyletic))))
}))
```

## Metrics summary

```{r, fig.height=3, fig.width=12}
plot_grid(
  # Parsimony
  PrettyHistogram(results.row$parsimony, 
                  vline = c(parsimony(treeRatchet, phydat), parsimony(treeUPGMA, phydat), parsimony(treeNJ, phydat)),
                  xlab = "parsimony score"),
  
  # Monophyletic TFs
  PrettyHistogram(results.row$nMonophyletic, 
                  vline = c(nMonophyletic(tf.expr.mask, treeRooted), nMonophyletic(tf.expr.mask, treeUPGMA), nMonophyletic(tf.expr.mask, treeNJ)), 
                  binwidth = 1, 
                  xlab = "# of monophyletic TFs"),
  
  # Mean bootstrap support
  PrettyHistogram(results.row$meanBS, 
                  vline = mean(treeRatchet$node.label), 
                  xlab = "parsimony support"),
  nrow = 1
)
```


```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=3.5}
# MP outperforms NJ and UPGMA in bootstrap support
boxplot(list(MP = treeBS$node.label, UPGMA = treeUPGMA$node.label, NJ = treeNJ$node.label))
title("bootstrap support values")
```

# Old stuff

```{r}
# Make ultrametric
treeUltra = phytools::force.ultrametric(treeRooted, method = "extend")

# Write to file
write.tree(treeUltra, "tf_analysis/treeUltra_v1.tree")

# Read dendrogram
dendro = DECIPHER::ReadDendrogram("tf_analysis/treeUltra_v1.tree")
```


```{r}
# Cut tree at every height
heights = get_branches_heights(dendro)
ident_matrix = t(unique(t(cutree(dendro, h = heights))))
ident_matrix.sorted = ident_matrix[match(colnames(tf.expr.mask), rownames(ident_matrix)),]

# Gini impurity of TFs at each unique grouping of OTs
tf.gini.summary = mclapply(tf.order, function(tf){
  lapply(colnames(ident_matrix.sorted), function(height){
    data.frame(tf = tf, 
               height = height, 
               gini = gini_impurities(data.table(group = as.character(ident_matrix.sorted[,height]), 
                                                 expr = as.character(tf.expr.mask[tf,])))[2,3])
  })
}, mc.cores = 16)

tf.gini.summary = do.call(rbind, unlist(tf.gini.summary, recursive = FALSE))

tf.gini.summary$height = as.numeric(tf.gini.summary$height)

PrettyHistogram(tf.gini.summary$GiniImpurity)

ggplot(tf.gini.summary, aes(x = height, y = GiniImpurity, label = tf))+
  geom_point() + 
  geom_text_repel()+
  theme_bw()
```

```{r, fig.height=7, fig.width=10}
gini_impurities(test)
```

## Big seurat

Find genes that are conserved in human and mouse (20394 orthologs, 17113 unique human genes)

```{r, eval = params$bigmatrix}
merge_1_2_3_4 = readRDS("../../Orthology/martMergeRefHuman.rds")
oneToOneMartHumanMouse = merge_1_2_3_4[merge_1_2_3_4$`Mouse homology type` == "ortholog_one2one",]
oneToOneMartHumanMouse$`Gene name`[oneToOneMartHumanMouse$`Gene name` == ""] = oneToOneMartHumanMouse$`Gene stable ID`[oneToOneMartHumanMouse$`Gene name` == ""]

# Generate the ortholog seurat object for each species
BigMatrixList = lapply(unique(bc_ortho$species), function(species) BigOrthologMatrix2(species, unique(oneToOneMartHumanMouse$`Gene name`), bc_ortho))
names(BigMatrixList) = unique(bc_ortho$species)
```

Make big matrix

```{r, eval = params$bigmatrix}
library(Matrix)
BigMatrix = Matrix(do.call(cbind, BigMatrixList), sparse = TRUE) 

# Order cells
BigMatrix = BigMatrix[,match(Cells(bc_ortho), colnames(BigMatrix))]
saveRDS(BigMatrix, "../../../storage/bcBigMatrix.rds")
# BigMatrix = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/VertebrateBigMatrix.rds")
BIGSEURAT = CreateSeuratObject(counts = BigMatrix[rownames(BigMatrix) != "",])
BIGSEURAT@meta.data = bc_ortho@meta.data
# BIGSEURAT@assays$RNA@data = BigMatrix[rownames(BigMatrix) != "",]
BIGSEURAT@assays$RNA@data[is.na(BIGSEURAT@assays$RNA@data)] <- 0
BIGSEURAT@assays$RNA@counts[is.na(BIGSEURAT@assays$RNA@counts)] <- 0
# BIGSEURAT = RenameCells(BIGSEURAT, new.names = paste0(BIGSEURAT$species, "_", Cells(BIGSEURAT)))
stopifnot(all(Cells(BIGSEURAT) %in% Cells(bc_ortho)))
Idents(BIGSEURAT) = "NOG"
saveRDS(BIGSEURAT, "../../../storage/vertebrateBC_BIGSEURAT.rds")
```

Add umap to BIGSEURAT

```{r}
BIGSEURAT = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateBC_BIGSEURAT.rds")
bc_ortho = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/BC_types_mammals_v6.rds")
BIGSEURAT@reductions$umap = bc_ortho@reductions$umap
DimPlotLabeled(BIGSEURAT, group.by = 'NOG')
saveRDS(BIGSEURAT, "../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateBC_BIGSEURAT.rds")
```

## TF analysis of bipolar cells

### Conservation scoring

```{r}
vertebrateBC.list = SplitObject(BIGSEURAT, split.by = "species")

# Scale within each species
features.use = rownames(BIGSEURAT)
scaled.list = lapply(vertebrateBC.list, function(object){
  
  # Average expression in each OT
  # average.data = AverageExpression(object, group.by = "seurat_clusters", assay = "RNA", features = features.use)$RNA
  
  # Average expression and then log transform
  average.data = AvgExpr(object, group.by = "seurat_clusters", assay = "RNA", features = features.use)
  
  # Scale each gene across OTs
  scaled.data = t(scale(t(average.data)))
  
  # Set NAs (features with no variance to 0)
  scaled.data[is.na(scaled.data)] = 0
  
  return(scaled.data)
})

# Reshape to be n_gene matrices of size n_species x n_OT
matrix.list = mclapply(features.use, function(feature) {
  do.call(gtools::smartbind, lapply(scaled.list, function(x) data.frame(as.list(x[feature,]))))
  }, mc.cores = 16)
names(matrix.list) = features.use

saveRDS(matrix.list, "../../Ortho_Objects/BC_OT_expression_log1p_scaled.rds")
```

```{r}
matrix.list = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/BC_OT_expression_log1p_scaled.rds")

bc.conservation.summary = data.frame(
  gene = rownames(BIGSEURAT),
  is.tf = toupper(rownames(BIGSEURAT)) %in% tf_genes,
  cor.score = sapply(matrix.list, function(matrix){
    matrix[is.na(matrix)] = 0 # Make zero to avoid NAs
    
    # Check that most species have non zero variance
    vars = apply(matrix, 1, var)
    if(length(which(vars == 0)) > nSpecies(bc_ortho)/2){
      cor.score = NA
    } else {
      cor = cor(t(matrix)) 
      
      # Remove NAs from zero variance rows
      cor.score = mean(na.omit(cor[upper.tri(cor)]))
    }
    return(cor.score)
  })
)

bc.conservation.summary$cor.score.rand = sapply(matrix.list, function(matrix){
  matrix[is.na(matrix)] = 0 # Make zero to avoid NAs
  
  # Shuffle rows
  matrix = ShuffleRows(matrix)
  
  # Check that most species have non zero variance
  vars = apply(matrix, 1, var)
  if(length(which(vars == 0)) > nSpecies(bc_ortho)/2){
    cor.score = NA
  } else {
    cor = cor(t(matrix)) 
    
    # Remove NAs from zero variance rows
    cor.score = mean(na.omit(cor[upper.tri(cor)]))
  }
  
  return(cor.score)
})

PrettyHistogram(bc.conservation.summary$cor.score, vline = c(max(bc.conservation.summary$cor.score.rand, na.rm = TRUE), 
                                                             min(bc.conservation.summary$cor.score.rand, na.rm = TRUE)))
PrettyHistogram(bc.conservation.summary$cor.score.rand, vline = c(max(bc.conservation.summary$cor.score, na.rm = TRUE), 
                                                             min(bc.conservation.summary$cor.score, na.rm = TRUE)))

# TFs have significantly higher cor.scores
ggboxplot(bc.conservation.summary, y = "cor.score", x = "is.tf")+
  geom_signif(
    comparisons = list(c("FALSE", "TRUE")),
    map_signif_level = TRUE
  )

saveRDS(bc.conservation.summary %>% arrange(desc(cor.score)), "../../Ortho_Objects/bc.conservation.summary.rds")
```

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=9}
bc.conservation.summary = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/bc.conservation.summary.rds")

cor.score.cutoff = 0.44
PrettyHistogram(bc.conservation.summary$cor.score, vline = cor.score.cutoff)

conserved.tfs = subset(bc.conservation.summary %>% arrange(desc(cor.score)), cor.score > cor.score.cutoff & is.tf)
tail(conserved.tfs)

# Examples of highly conserved TFs
lapply(head(conserved.tfs$gene, 5), function(gene) geneDotPlotFast(BIGSEURAT, gene, group.by = "NOG"))
lapply(tail(conserved.tfs$gene, 5), function(gene) geneDotPlotFast(BIGSEURAT, gene, group.by = "NOG"))
```

### Binarization

Save masks for inspection

```{r, eval = FALSE}
matrix.list = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/BC_OT_expression_log1p_scaled.rds")
full_masks = lapply(matrix.list[conserved.tfs$gene], BinarizeExpression, return.vector = FALSE)
names(full_masks) = conserved.tfs$gene

masks = lapply(full_masks, function(full_mask) {
  new = apply(full_mask, 2, mean) #BinarizeExpression(x, return.vector = TRUE)
  new[new > 0.5] = 1
  new[new <= 0.5] = 0
  return(new)
  })
names(masks) = conserved.tfs$gene

# Remove failures and TFs with no variance
which(is.na(masks))
variance = sapply(masks, var)
which(variance == 0)
filtered.masks = masks[!is.na(masks) & variance != 0]
tf.expr.mask = do.call(rbind, filtered.masks)
colnames(tf.expr.mask) = colnames(matrix.list[[1]])
saveRDS(tf.expr.mask, "../BC/tf_analysis/bc.tf.expr.mask.rds")

# Check PAX6 and TFAP2A
masks$ISL1
masks$VSX2
```


```{r, eval = FALSE}
pdf("../BC/tf_analysis/dotplots_masks.pdf", height = 9, width = 9)
for(gene in rownames(tf.expr.mask)){
  print(plot_grid(geneDotPlotFast(BIGSEURAT, gene, group.by = "NOG"), 
                  grid.grabExpr(draw(Heatmap(rbind(full_masks[[gene]], masks[[gene]])[,Metadata(BIGSEURAT, "NOG", "seurat_clusters")$seurat_clusters], 
                                             cluster_rows = F, 
                                             cluster_columns = F, 
                                             col = c("grey", "blue"), 
                                             column_names_side = "bottom", 
                                             row_names_side = "left"))), 
                  ncol = 1))
}
dev.off()
```

```{r, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
BIGSEURAT = readRDS("../../../storage/vertebrateBC_BIGSEURAT.rds")
```

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.5}
tf.order = rownames(tf.expr.mask)
tf.expr.mask = tf.expr.mask[tf.order, ]

Heatmap(t(tf.expr.mask),
```

### Maximum parsimony tree construction

```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
library(phangorn)
library(TreeTools)

set.seed(42)

phydat = phyDat(t(tf.expr.mask), type="USER", levels=c("0", "1"), compress = FALSE)

# Distance methods
treeUPGMA  <- upgma(dist(PhyDatToMatrix(phydat), method = "manhattan"))
treeNJ  <- NJ(dist(PhyDatToMatrix(phydat), method = "manhattan"))

# Error
# upgma_bootstrap = function(x) upgma(dist(PhyDatToMatrix(x), method = "manhattan"))
# NJ_bootstrap = function(x) NJ(dist(PhyDatToMatrix(x), method = "manhattan"))
# bs_upgma <- bootstrap.phyDat(phydat, FUN = upgma_bootstrap, bs = 100)
# bs_nj <- bootstrap.phyDat(phydat, FUN = NJ_bootstrap, bs = 100)

# Bootstrap myself 
bs_upgma = as.multiPhylo(lapply(seq_len(1000), function(iter) upgma(dist(PhyDatToMatrix(phydat)[,sample(1:length(tf.order), replace = TRUE)], method = "manhattan"))))
bs_nj = as.multiPhylo(lapply(seq_len(1000), function(iter) NJ(dist(PhyDatToMatrix(phydat)[,sample(1:length(tf.order), replace = TRUE)], method = "manhattan"))))

treeUPGMA = plotBS(treeUPGMA, bs_upgma, main="UPGMA")
treeNJ = plotBS(treeNJ, bs_nj, main="NJ")#, type = "unrooted")

# Maximum parsimony tree performs better than distance methods
# set.seed(42)
treeRatchet  <- pratchet(phydat, trace = 0, minit=500)
parsimony(treeRatchet, phydat)
parsimony(treeUPGMA, phydat)
parsimony(treeNJ, phydat)

# Assign branch lengths
treeRatchet  <- acctran(treeRatchet, phydat)
treeRatchet  <- di2multi(treeRatchet, tol = 1e-8)
if(inherits(treeRatchet, "multiPhylo")){
  treeRatchet <- unique(treeRatchet)
}

# Rooting tree for visualization
# treeRatchet <- root(phy = treeRatchet, node = 42)
# plot(treeRatchet, show.node.label = TRUE)
# add.scale.bar()

# Bootstrap support
plotBS(treeRatchet, type="unrooted", cex = 1, digits = 2)
# add.scale.bar()

# Labeling internal nodes
treeLabeled = treeRatchet
treeLabeled$node.label = (length(treeLabeled$tip.label)+1):((length(treeLabeled$tip.label)+treeLabeled$Nnode))
plot(treeLabeled, show.node.label = TRUE, type = "unrooted")
# add.scale.bar()

treeRooted = treeLabeled
# Rooting tree for visualization
# treeRooted <- root(phy = treeLabeled, node = 42)
# plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = TRUE)
```

```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
# Bootstrap
bs_mp = as.multiPhylo(mclapply(seq_len(100), function(iter) {
  phydat = phyDat(t(tf.expr.mask)[,sample(1:length(tf.order), replace = TRUE)], type="USER", levels=c("0", "1"), compress = FALSE)
  pratchet(phydat, trace = 0, minit=100)
}, mc.cores = 16))

treeBS = plotBS(treeRooted, bs_mp, type="unrooted", cex = 1, digits = 2)
```

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=3.5}
# does MP outperform NJ and UPGMA in bootstrap support?
boxplot(list(MP = treeBS$node.label, UPGMA = treeUPGMA$node.label, NJ = treeNJ$node.label))
title("bootstrap support values")
```

### Robustness of trees

Check 10 bootstrap trees and 10 identical MP trees

```{r, fig.height=7, fig.width=7}
id_mp = as.multiPhylo(mclapply(seq_len(100), function(iter) {
  phydat = phyDat(t(tf.expr.mask), type="USER", levels=c("0", "1"), compress = FALSE)
  pratchet(phydat, trace = 0, minit=100)
}, mc.cores = 16))

par(mfrow = c(5,1))
plot(bs_mp[1:10], type = "unrooted")
plot(id_mp[1:10], type = "unrooted")

plotBS(treeRooted, bs_mp, type = "unrooted")
plotBS(treeRooted, id_mp, type = "unrooted")
```

### Hyperparameter sweeps

The number of tfs to include is an arbitrary hyperparameter. Too few and we won't capture this complicated developmental cascade, but too many and we will introduce noise. Let's find the number of TFs that leads to the most self-consistent trees!

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=12}
sweep = seq(10, nrow(tf.expr.mask), by = 4)
nTF.trees = lapply(sweep, function(nTF) ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask)[,sample(1:nrow(tf.expr.mask), nTF)])) #[,1:nTF])) 
nTF.summary = do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(sweep), function(x) {
  rbind(data.frame(method = "UPGMA", bootstrap = nTF.trees[[x]]$UPGMA$node.label, nTF = sweep[x], parsimony = parsimony(nTF.trees[[x]]$UPGMA, nTF.trees[[x]]$phydat)), 
        data.frame(method = "NJ", bootstrap = nTF.trees[[x]]$NJ$node.label, nTF = sweep[x], parsimony = parsimony(nTF.trees[[x]]$NJ, nTF.trees[[x]]$phydat)), 
        data.frame(method = "MP", bootstrap = nTF.trees[[x]]$MP$node.label, nTF = sweep[x], parsimony = parsimony(nTF.trees[[x]]$MP, nTF.trees[[x]]$phydat)))
  }))

# Boxplot
ggplot(nTF.summary, aes(x = as.character(nTF), y = bootstrap, fill = method))+
    geom_boxplot(linetype = "dashed", outlier.shape = NA) +
    stat_boxplot(aes(ymin = ..lower.., ymax = ..upper..), outlier.shape = NA) +
    stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymin = ..ymax..), width = 0.5) +
    stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymax = ..ymin..), width = 0.5) +
    # ylab(ylab)+
    # ggtitle("Mixing of species")+
    facet_wrap(~method)+
    theme_cowplot()+
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())
```

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=12}
sweep = seq(1, nrow(tf.expr.mask)-15, by = 4)
nTF.trees = mclapply(sweep, function(nTF) ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask)[,nTF:(nTF+15)]), mc.cores = 2) #[,1:nTF])) 
nTF.summary = do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(sweep), function(x) {
  rbind(data.frame(method = "UPGMA", bootstrap = nTF.trees[[x]]$UPGMA$node.label, nTF = sweep[x], parsimony = parsimony(nTF.trees[[x]]$UPGMA, nTF.trees[[x]]$phydat)), 
        data.frame(method = "NJ", bootstrap = nTF.trees[[x]]$NJ$node.label, nTF = sweep[x], parsimony = parsimony(nTF.trees[[x]]$NJ, nTF.trees[[x]]$phydat)), 
        data.frame(method = "MP", bootstrap = nTF.trees[[x]]$MP$node.label, nTF = sweep[x], parsimony = parsimony(nTF.trees[[x]]$MP, nTF.trees[[x]]$phydat)))
  }))

# Boxplot
ggplot(nTF.summary, aes(x = as.factor(nTF), y = bootstrap, fill = method))+
    geom_boxplot(linetype = "dashed", outlier.shape = NA) +
    stat_boxplot(aes(ymin = ..lower.., ymax = ..upper..), outlier.shape = NA) +
    stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymin = ..ymax..), width = 0.5) +
    stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymax = ..ymin..), width = 0.5) +
    # ylab(ylab)+
    # ggtitle("Mixing of species")+
    facet_wrap(~method)+
    theme_cowplot()+
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())
```

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=15}
sweep = seq(10, nrow(tf.expr.mask), by = 1)
nTF.trees = mclapply(sweep, function(nTF) ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask)[,1:nTF]), mc.cores = 2)
nTF.summary = do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(sweep), function(x) {
  rbind(data.frame(method = "UPGMA", bootstrap = nTF.trees[[x]]$UPGMA$node.label, nTF = sweep[x], parsimony = parsimony(nTF.trees[[x]]$UPGMA, nTF.trees[[x]]$phydat)), 
        data.frame(method = "NJ", bootstrap = nTF.trees[[x]]$NJ$node.label, nTF = sweep[x], parsimony = parsimony(nTF.trees[[x]]$NJ, nTF.trees[[x]]$phydat)), 
        data.frame(method = "MP", bootstrap = nTF.trees[[x]]$MP$node.label, nTF = sweep[x], parsimony = parsimony(nTF.trees[[x]]$MP, nTF.trees[[x]]$phydat)))
  }))

# Boxplot
ggplot(nTF.summary, aes(x = as.factor(nTF), y = bootstrap, fill = method))+
    geom_boxplot(linetype = "dashed", outlier.shape = NA) +
    stat_boxplot(aes(ymin = ..lower.., ymax = ..upper..), outlier.shape = NA) +
    stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymin = ..ymax..), width = 0.5) +
    stat_boxplot(geom = "errorbar", aes(ymax = ..ymin..), width = 0.5) +
    facet_wrap(~method)+
    theme_cowplot()+
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.x = element_blank())
```

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=6}
ggplot(nTF.summary, aes(x = nTF, y = parsimony, color = method))+
  geom_line()+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 35, linetype = "dashed")+
  theme_cowplot()
```

Between 32 and 36 TFs

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=8}
trees.32 = ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask)[,1:32])
trees.31 = ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask)[,1:31])
trees.27 = ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask)[,1:27])
trees.22 = ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask)[,1:22])

trees.nosox5 = ConstructTrees(t(tf.expr.mask)[,-30])
```

Leave one out test

```{r, fig.height=5, fig.width=10}
loo.trees = mclapply(seq_len(ncol(t(tf.expr.mask))), function(col) {
  phydat = phyDat(t(tf.expr.mask)[,-col], type="USER", levels=c("0", "1"), compress = FALSE)
  tree = pratchet(phydat, trace=0, minit=100)
  list(TF = rownames(tf.expr.mask)[col], tree = tree, parsimony = parsimony(tree, phydat))
}, mc.cores = 16)

loo.summary = do.call(rbind, lapply(loo.trees, function(x) data.frame(TF = x$TF, parsimony = x$parsimony)))
loo.summary$TF = factor(loo.summary$TF, levels = loo.summary$TF)
ggplot(loo.summary, aes(x = TF, y = parsimony))+
  geom_col()+
  theme_cowplot()+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 66)+
  RotatedAxis()

boxplot(lapply(loo.trees, function(x) x$tree$node.label))
```


```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
loo.tree = as.multiPhylo(lapply(loo.trees, function(x) x$tree))
plot(loo.tree, type = "unrooted")
plotBS(treeRooted, loo.tree, type = "unrooted")
```

### Reconstruction of developmental sequence

```{r, fig.height=6.5, fig.width=6.5}
anc.acctran <- ancestral.pars(treeRooted, phydat, "ACCTRAN")
# anc.mpr <- ancestral.pars(treeRooted, phydat, "MPR")

pdf("../BC/tf_analysis/reconstruction_v2_acctran.pdf", height = 6.5, width = 6.5)
for(gene in tf.order){
  # plotAnc(treeLabeled, anc.mpr, which(tf.order == gene), type = "phylogram", cex.pie = 0.4, col = c("white", "black"))
  plotAnc(treeRooted, anc.acctran, which(tf.order == gene), type = "unrooted", cex.pie = 0.3, col = c("white", "black"), cex = 0.6, show.node.label = FALSE)
  title(gene)
}
dev.off()
```

Find TF switches

```{r, fig.height=30, fig.width=20}
gained = apply(treeRooted$edge, 1, function(row){
  gained = tf.order[which(ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[2]]][,2]) - ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2]) > 0)] #& anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2] == 0)] # an increase from 0 (not expressed)
  if(length(gained) > 0){
    gained = paste0(gained, "+")
    paste0(gained, collapse = "\n")
  } else {
    NA
  }
})

lost = apply(treeRooted$edge, 1, function(row){
  lost = tf.order[which(ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[2]]][,2]) - ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2]) < 0)] #& anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2] == 1)] # a decrease from 1 (expressed)
  if(length(lost) > 0){
    lost = paste0(lost, "-")
    paste0(lost, collapse = "\n")
  } else {
    NA
  }
})

# Plot gained TFs
pdf("../BC/tf_analysis/labeled_tree_gained_lost_v2.pdf", height=30, width=20)
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = TRUE, type = "unrooted")
edgelabels(gained, srt = 0, bg = "green")

# Plot lost TFs
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = TRUE, type = "unrooted")
edgelabels(lost, srt = 0, bg = "red")
dev.off()

# Plot all TF switches
switches = gsub('^\n|\n$', '', gsub("NA", "", paste0(gained, "\n", lost)))
switches[switches == "\n"] = NA

pdf("../BC/tf_analysis/labeled_tree_v2.pdf", height = 30, width = 20)
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = FALSE, type = "unrooted")
edgelabels(switches, srt = 0, bg = "white")
add.scale.bar()
dev.off()
```

### Overlay of experimentally derived birth order

```{r}
birth.order = c("BC4" = 1, "BC1A" = 2, "BC1B" = 3, "BC2" = 4, "BC3A" = 4, "BC3B" = 5, "RBC" = 6, 
                "BC8/9" = 7, "BC5D" = 7, "BC5B" = 8, "BC6" = 8, "BC5C" = 9, "BC5A" = 10, "BC7" = 11)
```

```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
library(ggtree)
library(treeio)

# Seed for tree layout
set.seed(5)
tree = as.treedata(treeRooted)

# Add metadata
# metadata = as.tibble(Metadata(bc_ortho, feature.1 = "type", feature.2 = "classification") %>% setNames(c("label", "classification")))
metadata = as_tibble(data.frame(label = names(birth.order), birth.order = birth.order))
expression = as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, lapply(anc.acctran, function(x) as.character(x[,2]))) %>% magrittr::set_colnames(tf.order))
expression$label = rownames(expression)
bootstrap = data.frame(label = as.character(treeRooted$node.label), 
                       bootstrap = treeBS$node.label)
anno_tree = list(as.tibble(tree), as.tibble(expression), metadata, bootstrap) %>% purrr::reduce(full_join, by = "label") 

# Names
anno_tree$name = anno_tree$label
anno_tree$name[ncol(tf.expr.mask):length(anno_tree$name)] = ""
saveRDS(anno_tree, "../BC/tf_analysis/bc_anno_tree.rds")
```


```{r, fig.height=7, fig.width=7}
# birth order
ggtree(as.treedata(anno_tree), layout = "equal_angle") + 
  geom_tippoint(aes(color=birth.order), size = 4)+
  scale_color_gradient(low = "yellow", high ="red")+
  # geom_nodepoint(aes(color=TCF4))+
  # scale_color_manual(values = c("black", "red"))+
  geom_tiplab(geom = "text")+
  # geom_text_repel(aes(label=name), max.overlaps = Inf, show.legend = FALSE, seed = 42)+
  geom_treescale()

# Bootstrap support values
ggtree(as.treedata(anno_tree), layout = "equal_angle") + 
  geom_nodepoint(aes(color=bootstrap), size = 4)+
  # geom_label(aes(label = bootstrap))+
  scale_color_gradient(low = "yellow", high ="red")+
  geom_tiplab(geom = "text")+
  # geom_text_repel(aes(label=name), max.overlaps = Inf, show.legend = FALSE, seed = 42)+
  geom_treescale()
```

Root based on earliest born type BC4

```{r, fig.height=6.5, fig.width=6.5}
save = treeRooted
treeRooted = root(treeRooted, outgroup = "BC4")

anc.acctran <- ancestral.pars(treeRooted, phydat, "ACCTRAN")

gained = apply(treeRooted$edge, 1, function(row){
  gained = tf.order[which(ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[2]]][,2]) - ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2]) > 0)] #& anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2] == 0)] # an increase from 0 (not expressed)
  if(length(gained) > 0){
    gained = paste0(gained, "+")
    paste0(gained, collapse = "\n")
  } else {
    NA
  }
})

lost = apply(treeRooted$edge, 1, function(row){
  lost = tf.order[which(ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[2]]][,2]) - ceiling(anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2]) < 0)] #& anc.acctran[[row[1]]][,2] == 1)] # a decrease from 1 (expressed)
  if(length(lost) > 0){
    lost = paste0(lost, "-")
    paste0(lost, collapse = "\n")
  } else {
    NA
  }
})

# Plot gained TFs
pdf("../BC/tf_analysis/labeled_tree_gained_lost_v2.pdf", height=30, width=20)
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = TRUE, type = "phylogram")
edgelabels(gained, srt = 0, bg = "green")

# Plot lost TFs
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = TRUE, type = "phylogram")
edgelabels(lost, srt = 0, bg = "red")
dev.off()

# Plot all TF switches
switches = gsub('^\n|\n$', '', gsub("NA", "", paste0(gained, "\n", lost)))
switches[switches == "\n"] = NA
```


```{r, fig.height=6.5, fig.width=6.5}
pdf("../BC/tf_analysis/labeled_tree_v2.pdf", height = 30, width = 20)
plot(treeRooted, show.node.label = FALSE)
edgelabels(switches, srt = 0, bg = "white")
add.scale.bar()
dev.off()
```

## Gene expression tree

```{r, eval = params$expr_corr, fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
# For consistency between RNA and integrated assay, we use same 2000 variable genes identified by integrated analysis
features.use = bc_ortho@assays$integrated@var.features
```

```{r type-cor, fig.height = 5, fig.width = 7}
bc_ortho$classification = ifelse(bc_ortho$NOG %in% c("BC1A", "BC1B", "BC2", "BC3A", "BC3B", "BC4"), "OFF", "ON")
avg_exp = AvgExpr(bc_ortho, verbose = FALSE, group.by = "NOG", features = features.use, assay = "RNA")
type_cor = cor(avg_exp)
ha = HeatmapAnnotation(subclass = Metadata(bc_ortho, feature.1 = "NOG", feature.2 = "classification")$classification, 
                   annotation_name_side = "left",
                   col = list(subclass = c("OFF" = "orange", "ON" = "violet")))
# top_ha = HeatmapAnnotation(OT = colnames(type_cor), annotation_name_side = "right")

hm = Heatmap(type_cor, name = "cor", 
        # col = colorRamp2(c(0.5, 1), c("white", "red")), 
        cluster_rows = hclust(as.dist(1 - type_cor), method = "average"),
        cluster_columns = hclust(as.dist(1 - type_cor), method = "average"),
        # clustering_distance_rows = "pearson",
        # clustering_distance_columns = "pearson",
        show_column_names = TRUE, show_column_dend = FALSE, 
        column_title = "pairwise similarity between OTs (un-integrated)",
        show_row_names = TRUE, row_dend_side = "left", row_names_side = "left",
        heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Correlation"), 
        top_annotation = ha)

ggarrange(grid.grabExpr(draw(hm)))

# ggsave("../../figures/my_figs/type-cor-1.png", height = 10, width = 12)
# type_order = dendextend::order.hclust(hclust(as.dist(1 - type_cor)))

# Add dendrogram order to object
# vertebrateAC$dendro.order = factor(vertebrateAC$seurat_clusters, levels = rev(type_order))
# saveRDS(vertebrateAC, "../../Ortho_Objects/vertebrateAC_v3_100.rds")
```

BC3B is a little similar to BC5

### pvclust robustness check

We indeed find that ON and OFF bipolars are two different subclasses with significant au p-values. 

```{r}
# bc.pv <- pvclust(avg_exp, nboot=100)

pvrect(bc.pv, alpha=0.95)
```

## non-TF tree

Is this discrepancy of BC3B/BC4 specific to TFs

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=9}
bc.conservation.summary = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/bc.conservation.summary.rds")

cor.score.cutoff = 0.44
PrettyHistogram(bc.conservation.summary$cor.score, vline = cor.score.cutoff)

conserved.nontfs.all = subset(bc.conservation.summary %>% arrange(desc(cor.score)), cor.score > cor.score.cutoff & !is.tf)
```


```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=9}
# Random sample of same number of nonTFs with same cor.score distribution
# hist(conserved.nontfs.all$cor.score)
# hist(conserved.tfs$cor.score)

breaks = seq(0.4, 1, by = 0.2)
hist.tf = hist(conserved.tfs$cor.score, breaks = breaks)
hist.nontf = hist(conserved.nontfs.all$cor.score, breaks = breaks)
bias = hist.tf$density/hist.nontf$density
conserved.nontfs.all$sampling.group = cut(conserved.nontfs.all$cor.score, breaks = breaks, labels = breaks[-1]) #ceiling(conserved.nontfs.all$cor.score*10)/10
conserved.nontfs.all$sampling.weight = as.numeric(bias[match(as.character(conserved.nontfs.all$sampling.group), as.character(breaks[-1]))])

biased.sample = sample(1:nrow(conserved.nontfs.all), nrow(conserved.tfs), prob = conserved.nontfs.all$sampling.weight)
hist(biased.sample)
conserved.nontfs = conserved.nontfs.all[biased.sample,] %>% arrange(desc(cor.score))
hist(conserved.nontfs$cor.score, breaks = breaks)

# Examples of highly conserved TFs
# lapply(head(conserved.nontfs$gene, 5), function(gene) geneDotPlotFast(BIGSEURAT, gene, group.by = "NOG"))
# lapply(tail(conserved.nontfs$gene, 5), function(gene) geneDotPlotFast(BIGSEURAT, gene, group.by = "NOG"))
```

Check that cor.scores are similar between TF and nonTF sample

```{r}
boxplot(list(nontf = conserved.nontfs$cor.score, tf = conserved.tfs$cor.score))
wilcox.test(conserved.nontfs$cor.score, conserved.tfs$cor.score)
wilcox.test(conserved.nontfs.all$cor.score, conserved.nontfs$cor.score)

saveRDS(conserved.nontfs, "../BC/tf_analysis/conserved.nontfs.rds")
```


```{r, eval = FALSE}
conserved.nontfs = readRDS("../BC/tf_analysis/conserved.nontfs.rds")
matrix.list = readRDS("../../Ortho_Objects/BC_OT_expression_log1p_scaled.rds")
full_masks = lapply(matrix.list[conserved.nontfs$gene], BinarizeExpression, return.vector = FALSE)
names(full_masks) = conserved.nontfs$gene

masks = lapply(full_masks, function(full_mask) {
  new = apply(full_mask, 2, mean) #BinarizeExpression(x, return.vector = TRUE)
  new[new > 0.5] = 1
  new[new <= 0.5] = 0
  return(new)
  })
names(masks) = conserved.nontfs$gene

# Remove failures and TFs with no variance
which(is.na(masks))
variance = sapply(masks, var)
which(variance == 0)
filtered.masks = masks[!is.na(masks) & variance != 0]
nontf.expr.mask = do.call(rbind, filtered.masks)
colnames(nontf.expr.mask) = colnames(matrix.list[[1]])
saveRDS(nontf.expr.mask, "../BC/tf_analysis/bc.nontf.expr.mask.rds")

# Checks
```


```{r, eval = FALSE}

for(gene in rownames(nontf.expr.mask)){
  print(plot_grid(geneDotPlotFast(BIGSEURAT, gene, group.by = "NOG"), 
                  grid.grabExpr(draw(Heatmap(rbind(full_masks[[gene]], masks[[gene]])[,Metadata(BIGSEURAT, "NOG", "seurat_clusters")$seurat_clusters], 
                                             cluster_rows = F, 
                                             cluster_columns = F, 
                                             col = c("grey", "blue"), 
                                             column_names_side = "bottom", 
                                             row_names_side = "left"))), 
                  ncol = 1))
}
dev.off()
```
